concepts.title: Subjectivity as an Obstacle to Reduction

bodyFields.claim: The irreducible subjectivity of consciousness is an obstacle to many proposed solutions to the mind-body problem.
bodyFields.support: The bat example demonstrates that we cannot know what it is like for the bat, revealing a real aspect of experience beyond physiological or behavioral description, which undermines reductionist accounts.

concepts.title: What It's Like

bodyFields.definition: The subjective character of conscious experience, specifying what it is like to be a particular creature (e.g., a bat).
bodyFields.significance: Highlights the limits of objective physical sciences in capturing consciousness, indicating that the physical description of the natural order is incomplete.

relatedConceptNames: Subjectivity as an Obstacle to Reduction

concepts.title: Mind-Body Problem

bodyFields.description: The philosophical problem of the relationship between mental phenomena and physical states, especially whether consciousness can be reduced to physical processes.
bodyFields.components: Subjective consciousness, objective physical description, reduction, and the psychophysical nexus.

relatedConceptNames: Subjectivity as an Obstacle to Reduction
